title: crotalaria medicaginea
intro: Crotalaria medicaginea, commonly known as stickseed, is a plant species in the family Fabaceae, native to tropical and subtropical regions of Australia, Africa, and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Crotalaria medicaginea include reducing fever, relieving digestive problems, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Crotalaria medicaginea include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used externally or internally, depending on the specific health concern being addressed.
